topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The printout lay on the kitchen counter, a stark white sheet mocking the warmth of the morning sunlight streaming through the window. My fingers traced the unfamiliar lettering, the genetic markers a cold, undeniable narrative laid bare. It wasn't a revelation; it was an indictment. Every line seemed to point to a lineage I never knew, a history I was suddenly burdened with. I crumpled the paper slowly, the sound of the tearing sound loud in the silence of the apartment. When my sister walked in, humming off-key, I quickly shoved the evidence under a stack of mail, my jaw tight, forcing a smile that felt brittle around the edges.

[story 2]

The lab results had arrived tucked inside a plain envelope, the official seal feeling heavy and damning in my palm. I sat on the edge of my bed, staring at the complex chart, the biological percentages blurring into a terrifying mosaic. A knot tightened in my chest, a physical constriction that made drawing a full breath difficult. I kept my gaze fixed on the ceiling, meticulously focusing on the pattern of the plaster, anywhere but the numbers detailing where I truly came from. When my mother called, her voice bright and cheerful, I managed a clipped, distant response, the sound barely escaping my throat as I pretended to be engrossed in a book.

[story 3]

I stood in the hallway, the fluorescent light buzzing overhead, the paper clutched so tightly my knuckles were white. The information confirmed what I had always suspected but never dared to confront: I wasn't entirely mine. A wave of profound unease washed over me, making my stomach churn. I walked toward the bathroom, the tiles cool beneath my bare feet, and stared at my reflection. The person staring back looked unfamiliar, overlaid with a strange, unwanted ancestry. I turned abruptly, unable to face the mirror, and retreated to my room, pulling the door shut with a decisive, almost violent click.
